methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Dronamics is a Series A-stage drone logistics company founded by Svilen and Konstantin Rangelov. The target operates within the Drones & Autonomous sector, which includes 8 private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Rainmaker among them) and no direct public comparables. The narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier proved insufficient for reliable median-based pricing.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology given data constraints. Using total capital raised to date ($43.2M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields $86.4M. This method accounts for expected value creation between funding rounds while remaining grounded in observed capital deployment. A ±15% sensitivity band around this point estimate produces a low of $73.4M and high of $99.4M.

The triangulated valuation range of $73–99M (median $86M) reflects limited methodological depth; only stage-progression analysis is sufficiently defensible given peer-set constraints. Key uncertainties include market traction data, unit economics, and regulatory pathway clarity for commercial drone operations—factors that would typically narrow valuation dispersion in a fuller comparable analysis. Any material valuation refinement depends on obtaining similar-stage peer transaction data or detailed financial metrics from Dronamics itself.
